executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, and Kids & Company childcare. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay groups. Separately, Canadian cyber authorities have issued an updated alert for critical Cisco ASA and firewall vulnerabilities that organizations must patch immediately.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a massive ransomware attack wave affecting over 100 organizations across all sectors in the past 48 hours. CCCS has issued Alert AL25-012 (Update 2) for critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate patching. Major incidents include high-profile consumer brands, public health agencies, municipalities, school boards, and a confirmed database leak at National Money Mart.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer services) compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware group. Active Green + Ross (consumer services) compromised by Sinobi ransomware group.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi ransomware group. Trucash compromised by Lynx ransomware group.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's CCCS issued four official alerts in the past two weeks, including a critical Linux vulnerability (CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500), a cPanel flaw actively exploited (CVE-2026-41940), and a warning about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS platforms. Ivanti EPMM zero-day (CVE-2026-6973) is under active exploitation with a federal patch deadline set for four days. ShinyHunters ransomware group breached Canvas LMS affecting 9,000+ schools and 275 million users globally, disrupting finals week. Major breaches reported at Trellix (source code), Zara (197,000 records), NVIDIA GeForce NOW (Armenian users), and four U.S. healthcare providers (3+ million patients).

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued four official advisories this period: two Linux vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500, CVE-2026-31431), actively exploited cPanel flaw (CVE-2026-41940), and a social engineering alert targeting enterprise SaaS environments. No specific Canadian retail or financial institution breaches were identified in reporting, though global threat activity remains elevated.
